name: Loki
shortDescription: Loki is a Norse god associated with mischief, trickery, and shapeshifting. He is a complex figure who is both a helper and an adversary to the gods.
description: Loki is a complex and contradictory figure in Norse mythology. He is often described as a trickster god, associated with mischief, lies, and shapeshifting. His parentage is debated, with some accounts identifying him as the son of Fárbauti, a jötunn (giant), and Laufey (also known as Nál). This mixed heritage places him in a liminal space between the gods (Aesir) and the giants, a position that contributes to his ambiguous role in the Norse cosmos. Loki is capable of changing his shape and gender, frequently using these abilities to deceive and manipulate. He is involved in numerous pivotal events in Norse mythology, including the birth of Sleipnir (Odin's eight-legged horse, whom Loki mothered), the theft of Thor's hammer Mjolnir, and the death of Baldr. While Loki sometimes aids the gods, his actions often have negative consequences, ultimately leading to Ragnarok, the twilight of the gods. He is prophesied to play a key role in the battle, fighting against the Aesir. After Ragnarok, it is unclear what becomes of Loki, but some accounts suggest he is eventually defeated. He is a powerful and disruptive force, embodying chaos and challenging the established order of the Aesir.
